description:
Wire up the Stripe SDK with a single-entrypoint client and codify the **"no PHI in Stripe"** policy that everything downstream depends on.

**Scope**
- `src/lib/stripe/client.ts` — single Stripe SDK instance (server-only). All Stripe calls route through this module or its siblings (`customer.ts`, `subscription.ts`).
- Secrets live in AWS Secrets Manager (`apoth/<env>/stripe/restricted_key`). Local dev uses a test-mode restricted key with the minimum scopes needed.
- Environment sentinel: assert at boot that `STRIPE_ENV ∈ {test, live}` matches `APP_ENV`. Hard-exit on mismatch.
- **BAA policy** (Stripe does not sign a BAA — codified in `docs/stripe-data-policy.md` and lint-enforced):
  - Customer metadata: only `apoth_user_id` (opaque ULID).
  - Product/price names: opaque internal codes (e.g. `tx-cond-a-monthly`). Patient-facing names render from our DB.
  - Invoice line items: opaque descriptors only.
  - No condition names, no diagnoses, no DOB, no SSN, no health data anywhere in Stripe.

**Acceptance**
- `src/lib/stripe/client.ts` is the only path to the Stripe SDK (grep audit).
- A failing test exists that catches any new code passing a condition name or PHI-shaped string to Stripe (regex guard on the policy doc).
title: Stripe SDK setup + BAA-aware metadata policy
